text.title: resource-management Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for resource-management as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent over-allocates top performers because they have the highest skill scores.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hard-cap any resource at 85% in the allocation engine. Top performers are also the highest attrition risk when consistently overloaded.

failure-mode.description: Static resource plans not reconciled against actuals for more than two weeks.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Allocation plans go stale in 2–4 weeks. An unreconciled plan produces over-confidence in capacity estimates that do not reflect real availability.

failure-mode.description: Skill matching by free-text similarity rather than explicit skill keys from roster.yaml.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Embedding-based skill matching from job descriptions produces plausible but unverified matches. Only roster.yaml is authoritative; reject any match not grounded in a roster entry.
